edges.ev: vt2 encodes a co-ortholog of TRYPTOPHAN AMINOTRANSFERASE OF ARABIDOPSIS1 (TAA1), which converts Trp to indole-3-pyruvic acid
spi1 encodes a flavin monooxygenase similar to the YUCCA (YUC) genes involved in local auxin biosynthesis
Auxin binds TIR1 to target BIF1/BIF4 for degradation
TIR1-dependent degradation of BIF1/BIF4 Aux/IAAs removes repression
TIR1-dependent degradation of BIF4 Aux/IAA
BIF1 and BIF4 directly control BA1 transcription; Bif1/Bif4 dominant shows no BA1 expression
BIF1 and BIF4 directly control BA1 transcription
ARF4, ARF16, ARF27, ARF29, and ARF34 bound to all four probes of BA1 regulatory region
ba1 mutants lack all axillary meristems; BA1 is required for AM initiation
BIF2 phosphorylates ZmPIN1a and regulates ZmPIN1a subcellular localization in vivo
BIF2 and BA1 co-localize in the nucleus; BIF2 functions by interacting with BA1 to promote axillary meristem initiation
ZmPIN1a encodes putative auxin efflux carrier with basal membrane polarization directing basipetal auxin flux
ZmPIN1b encodes putative auxin efflux carrier
NPA treatment (PAT inhibition) completely abolished ability of IM to form any axillary meristem
AM initiation is prerequisite to SPM formation and kernel row establishment
Feedback regulation of CK biosynthesis; IPT catalyses the first committed step in CK biosynthesis
IPT is the rate-limiting enzyme for cytokinin biosynthesis
CKX catalyses irreversible degradation of cytokinins and modulates cellular CK levels
Cytokinin oxidases catalyse irreversible degradation of cytokinins
CK signalling is perceived by histidine kinase receptors at cell surface
CK signalling involves His-Asp phosphotransfer through AHPs
AHP transfers phosphate to nuclear type-B response regulators
ABPH1 transcription is CK-inducible, downstream of type-B ARRs
Type-A ARR feedback negatively modulates CK->WUS activation

perturbations.ev: vt2 tassels lack branches and spikelets entirely; ears show severe kernel reduction
spi1 tassels have fewer branches and spikelets, and spi1 ears are small with fewer kernels
spi1;vt2 doubles slightly more severe than vt2 alone
Tassels with reduced branches and spikelets; ears shortened with disorganized kernel rows
bif2 mutants show fewer branches and spikelets
bif4 heterozygous similar defects to bif1
Pin-like inflorescences in which organogenesis was often completely impaired
ba1 mutants completely lack all axillary meristems, resulting in a sterile plant
NPA treatment completely abolished ability of IM to form any axillary meristem
abph1 shows decussate phyllotaxy and enlarged SAM; KRN not directly scored
Exogenous CK treatment to ear primordia increases meristem size
Exogenous auxin treatment promotes lateral primordia initiation
